articles.description: The Clifton, by Autograph Group, offers the benefits of lock-and-leave living while maintaining the feel of a larger, more traditional home.

articles.title: NASA orders astronauts to take shelter after new leak aboard the International Space Station
articles.description: NASA on Friday ordered astronauts to take shelter after a fresh leak was detected aboard the International Space Station. The five astronauts moved into the SpaceX capsule that is docked at the station while cosmonauts worked to fix the leak, which is on the Russian side of the orbiting laboratory.

articles.title: Astronauts briefly take shelter during repair to fix leak on the International Space Station
articles.description: NASA on Friday temporarily ordered astronauts to take shelter during repairs to fix a fresh leak aboard the International Space Station. articles.title: O-Train lines 2 and 4 to shut for maintenance
articles.description: OC Transpo's new GM says the shutdown of Line 2 and the Line 4 spur to the airport will allow "proactive maintenance activities."

articles.title: The DOGE Boys Get VC Funding to Support Their Latest Enterprise
articles.description: Former DOGE members and Elon Musk allies are backing a startup aimed at using AI to apply “learnings” from DOGE to the private sector.

articles.title: Has Microsoft Lost Its Mojo (Again)?
articles.description: Microsoft’s AI products aren’t selling, and Github’s been plagued with troubles. WIRED spoke with VP Scott Hanselman about whether the company is in catch-up mode.

articles.title: Could B.C. see its first Conservative premier in 100 years?
articles.description: The Conservative Party of British Columbia hasn’t won an election since 1926, and hasn’t won a seat in 50 years. As recently as the 2020 provincial election, the party earned just 36,000 votes. But in 2024, the party surged in popularity, nearly toppling the long-thought invincible B.C.
